description:
You are a web developer with a small collection of static files already living in:

/home/user/site/static/

Inside that directory (and its sub-directories) there are JavaScript, CSS and PNG files.  
Your goal is to automate the creation of a sorted “manifest” of those assets so that a deployment system can quickly see every file that must be copied to a CDN.

1. Write a shell script called /home/user/site/generate_asset_list.sh.  
   • The script may be as short as 2 – 3 commands (not counting the she-bang).  
   • It must search *recursively* under /home/user/site/static/ for files whose
     names end in .js, .css or .png (case-sensitive).  
   • Every matching path must be written to /home/user/site/asset-list.txt
     using **forward-slash paths that are *relative* to /home/user/site**
     (e.g. “static/app.js”, not “/home/user/site/static/app.js”).  
   • The resulting lines must be sorted alphabetically (the normal POSIX sort
     order is fine).  
   • There must be exactly one file path per line, and the file must end with
     a single trailing newline character.

2. Make the script executable and run it.  
   Running the script should be idempotent; executing it multiple times must
   always yield the same /home/user/site/asset-list.txt for the current set of
   files.

3. Do **not** echo any extra text to the console except what the commands
   naturally output.  The automated tests will only examine the contents of
   /home/user/site/asset-list.txt.

Expected output file format (verbatim, including final newline):

static/app.js
static/css/style.css
static/images/logo.png
static/utils/helper.js

Once the script has run successfully and the file exists with the exact
contents shown above, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial filesystem that exists *before* the agent starts:

/home/user/site/static/app.js
  └─ Content: console.log("App main");\n

/home/user/site/static/utils/helper.js
  └─ Content: export function help() { return true; }\n

/home/user/site/static/css/style.css
  └─ Content: body { margin: 0; }\n

/home/user/site/static/images/logo.png
  └─ Content: (binary PNG stub, any bytes are acceptable)

File/directory permissions: everything is rwx for the owner (user).

Verification criteria for the grader:

1. A file called /home/user/site/asset-list.txt exists.  
2. Its exact byte content (including the trailing newline) is:
   static/app.js\n
   static/css/style.css\n
   static/images/logo.png\n
   static/utils/helper.js\n
3. Line ordering is strictly alphabetical; any deviation (including additional
   whitespace or missing final newline) fails the test.
